title: Child Labor Detection And Supply Chain Risk In Global Agriculture
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where child labor risk is highest in agricultural supply chains, linking household-survey data, crop-production maps, and supply-chain traceability data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: ILO and UNICEF estimate 160 million children in child labor, 70 percent in agriculture. Cocoa, coffee, cotton, and palm oil supply chains face regulatory pressure from EU and US forced-labor bans. But sub-national data on where child labor occurs and which supply chains are implicated is fragmentary, making enforcement and remediation targeting imprecise.

success_metrics.target: Every child-labor risk estimate links survey, crop, and supply-chain data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
